kelp-bass.cast: Tight to boiler rock & kelp edges, 20–40 ft; fan the up-current edge

rigs.name: Weedless paddletail swimbait
rigs.detail: 5–7" (MC / Big Hammer / Kicker) on a 1/2–3/4 oz weighted weedless hook. Burn it over the kelp; don't swing on the bite — keep grinding till it loads.

rigs.name: Leadhead swimbait
rigs.detail: 5" Big Hammer on 1/2–1 oz head; count it down along rock edges, vary the sink.

rigs.name: Surface iron
rigs.detail: Tady/Salas over the shallowest part of the reef at first light; brisk retrieve.

barred-sand-bass.cast: Fan-cast sandy flats & reef edges; retrieve near bottom

regs.season: Year-round · spawning aggregation closures may apply

rigs.name: Leadhead swimbait, slow-rolled
rigs.detail: 4–5" plastic on 1/2–3/4 oz; stop-and-drop along the bottom. If one bites, more are there.

rigs.name: Carolina-rigged plastic
rigs.detail: 3/4–1 oz sliding sinker, grub or swimbait dragged over sand/mud flats.

rigs.name: Flylined squid or anchovy
rigs.detail: Live bait on structure during the summer spawn; light line, patient drift.

spotted-bay-bass.cast: To pilings, eelgrass edges & channel drop-offs; work slow near bottom

rigs.name: Leadhead swimbait / dropshot
rigs.detail: 3–5" swimbait on 3/16–3/8 oz (or dropshot). Reaction bites on the fall around structure.

rigs.name: Curly-tail grub
rigs.detail: AA Worms / Big Hammer grub on a light fine-wire leadhead; rotate the bait when chewed.

rigs.name: Ned rig / small crank
rigs.detail: Finesse around current breaks and dock shade when they're picky.

largemouth-bass.cast: Points, submerged timber & windward bank; red-clay cliffs in 18–25 ft midday

regs.bag: 5/day state limit · Irvine Lake: catch-and-release only per lake rules

rigs.name: Wacky / Texas Senko
rigs.detail: Soft stickbait worked slow around shoreline cover and fallen timber — the dependable Irvine producer.

rigs.name: Dropshot, finesse plastic
rigs.detail: Off Rocky Point and the red-clay cliffs at 18–25 ft during bright daytime hours.

rigs.name: Spinnerbait / shad crank / topwater
rigs.detail: White spinnerbait or shad-pattern X-Rap at dawn, dusk and after dark — reaction. Bass are catch-and-release here.

spotfin-croaker.cast: Into the first trough/gutter, 20–50 ft, on a building high

rigs.name: Carolina rig, sand crab
rigs.detail: Sliding egg sinker, #4 hook, fresh soft-shell sand crab in the first trough. Soft mouth — set on the load, not a jerk.

rigs.name: High-low surf rig
rigs.detail: Two dropper loops with mussel or ghost shrimp; covers two depths at once.

rigs.name: Gulp! sandworm
rigs.detail: Camo 2" Gulp! on a dropper when crabs are scarce.

california-corbina.cast: Sight-cast: lead cruising fish 6–10 ft, drop into the whitewash

rigs.name: Light Carolina / swivel rig, sand crab
rigs.detail: Live soft-shell sand crab, #2–6, light egg sinker. Lead the fish, cast into breaking whitewater to hide the splash, then let the surf work the bait — don't move it.

rigs.name: Fly-lined sand crab (no weight)
rigs.detail: On an outgoing tide, let line out and drift the crab away from the beach — deadly.

rigs.name: Gulp! sandworm
rigs.detail: Camo sandworm in winter when crabs thin out; corbina rarely take hard lures.

pacific-bonito.cast: Long casts into surface boils at the harbor mouth, 60–100 ft

rigs.name: Cast jig / chrome
rigs.detail: 1 oz Krocodile or Tady 45 chrome; fast, erratic retrieve through the boil.

rigs.name: Flylined sardine / anchovy
rigs.detail: Live bait drifted to a working school; light line for more bites.

rigs.name: Small swimbait / feather
rigs.detail: 4" swimbait or a trolled feather when they're picky on chrome.

pacific-mackerel.cast: Drop straight down off pier/jetty pilings, or short casts to the school

rigs.name: Sabiki rig, tipped
rigs.detail: Size 4–6 sabiki with squid tips on the hooks; jig vertically. Best bait-maker at first light.

rigs.name: Small chrome jig
rigs.detail: Lucky Joe or tiny Krocodile when they're chasing on top.

rigs.name: Bait under a float
rigs.detail: Cut squid suspended for a hands-off setup off the pier.

barred-surfperch.cast: Into the trough just beyond the breakers, 20–40 ft; fish the foam seam

rigs.name: Carolina rig, sand crab
rigs.detail: Live or fresh sand crab, #4–6, light sliding sinker, drifted through the trough on the incoming. The #1 bait, hands down.

rigs.name: Hi-low dropper rig
rigs.detail: Two droppers with sand crab or Gulp! sandworm; great for locating schooled fish.

rigs.name: Small grub on leadhead
rigs.detail: 2" motor-oil/root-beer grub on a light head when fish are aggressive.

opaleye.cast: Short casts into rocky wash zones & tidepool edges; berley the spot

rigs.name: Float rig, frozen peas
rigs.detail: Small #8 hook under a float, baited with frozen peas or moss; chum a wash zone over rock to draw them up.

rigs.name: Light dropper, shellfish
rigs.detail: Ghost shrimp or mussel on a fine-wire hook tight to the rocks.

rigs.name: Bread / innkeeper worm
rigs.detail: Doughball or innkeeper worm when they're keyed on chum.

california-sheephead.cast: To reef edges & crevices; let it sit on the bottom, 20–50 ft

rigs.name: Dropper-loop, shellfish
rigs.detail: 1–2 oz, #1 hook with whole shrimp, mussel or crab parked on the bottom near reef structure. Big canines crush shellfish.

rigs.name: Live or cut squid
rigs.detail: On a leadhead or dropper down in the rocks — a sheephead magnet.

rigs.name: Rock crab / mussel
rigs.detail: Natural reef forage fished tight to crevices.

black-crappie.cast: To brush, docks & timber; suspend over the school and hold depth

rigs.name: Mini-jig under a float
rigs.detail: 1/16 oz white or glow tube/jig suspended over brush; count down to the school then hold that depth. A floating lantern at night pulls them in.

rigs.name: Live minnow / micro-swimbait
rigs.detail: Minnow under a slip float, or a small minnow-profile bait — crappie key on baitfish.

rigs.name: Small spinner / inline
rigs.detail: Tiny spinner or micro-crank slow-rolled past structure.

channel-catfish.cast: To the flats, west shore & dam; soak on the bottom, 30–70 ft out

rigs.name: Slip-sinker bottom rig, cut bait
rigs.detail: Carolina-style sliding sinker, 2/0 hook, cut mackerel or bonito on the bottom. Evening and night are best; the proven Irvine cat bait.

rigs.name: Nightcrawler / chicken liver
rigs.detail: Classic bottom soak on a sliding-sinker rig; will pick these up even in trout season.

rigs.name: Prepared / stink bait
rigs.detail: Catmando or dough bait on a treble for scent-tracking cats.

california-halibut.cast: Fan-cast sand flats & channel edges; drag slowly, 30–60 ft

rigs.name: Carolina rig, live bait or swimbait
rigs.detail: 1/2–3/4 oz sliding sinker with a live smelt/anchovy, or a Big Hammer/MS Slug dragged slow along the sand. Eat is a soft tap then weight — sweep-set, don't jerk.

rigs.name: Dropshot live bait
rigs.detail: Live smelt just off the bottom over sand flats; deadly on bigger flatties.

rigs.name: Crank / jerkbait near bottom
rigs.detail: In bays/harbors over clean sand with less grass; keep it ticking bottom.

yellowfin-croaker.cast: Into troughs and gutters along sandy/rocky beaches & harbor shallows at night

rigs.name: Ghost shrimp, light rig
rigs.detail: Live or fresh ghost shrimp on a #4–6 hook with a small egg sinker; very sensitive bite, use a stiff-tip rod and watch for the tap-tap-pull.

rigs.name: Hi-low surf rig, mussel
rigs.detail: Two droppers with fresh mussel or clam along the first trough; productive after dark.

rigs.name: Sand crab on a dropper
rigs.detail: Soft-shell or peeler sand crab on a single-hook dropper — also takes spotfin at the same spot.

white-croaker.cast: Drop down off any pier or jetty; very short or vertical presentations work best

rigs.name: Sabiki / small high-low rig
rigs.detail: Size 6–8 sabiki or two small droppers baited with cut squid or anchovy; just drop to the bottom and jig lightly. Prolific biter.

rigs.name: Small Carolina rig, cut squid
rigs.detail: 1/4 oz slider with a #6 hook and strip of squid; drag slowly on the sand bottom.

rigs.name: Nightcrawler on a dropper
rigs.detail: Works surprisingly well from piers at night; also picks up perch and small bass.

white-seabass.cast: Along kelp edges and channel drop-offs; slow presentations near bottom

rigs.name: Live squid, flylined
rigs.detail: The single best WSB bait — hook through the mantle and let it swim freely near the kelp edge or channel bottom at dawn. No weight.

rigs.name: Live mackerel / sardine
rigs.detail: Hooked through the nose on a 4/0–6/0 circle hook; drift or slow-troll along kelp walls.

rigs.name: Large swimbaits
rigs.detail: 8–10" soft swimbait on a 2–3 oz head worked slowly along the bottom near kelp — effective when live bait is unavailable.

california-scorpionfish.cast: Drop straight down over reef and rocky structure; no movement needed
california-scorpionfish.note: Venomous spines — handle with a wet rag or pliers. Delicious table fish.

rigs.name: Bottom dropper, live shrimp or squid
rigs.detail: 1–2 oz weight, #1–2/0 hook, live or fresh ghost shrimp or cut squid placed directly on the bottom over rocky structure. Scorpion fish ambush — they don't chase bait.

rigs.name: Shrimp fly or small leadhead
rigs.detail: Small shrimp-pattern fly or 1/4 oz leadhead with a curly tail right on the reef; lift and drop slowly.

rigs.name: Chunk of mackerel or squid
rigs.detail: Any oily bottom bait parked on the rocks will eventually find a sculpin.

blue-rockfish.cast: Mid-water over the kelp canopy; vertical jigging or drift bait works well

regs.season: Verify CDFW regs before fishing — seasonal and depth closures apply

rigs.name: Dropper loop rig, cut squid
rigs.detail: Two-hook dropper rig with strips of squid or anchovy; drift mid-water above the kelp canopy — blue rockfish school here and bite readily.

rigs.name: Small metal jig
rigs.detail: 1/2–1 oz diamond jig or chrome butterfly jig jigged vertically through the school.

rigs.name: Small swimbait
rigs.detail: 3–4" paddle-tail on a 1/4–1/2 oz head worked through the mid-water column.

rainbow-trout.cast: Near inlet streams, dam face & open water; troll or soak near the surface in winter
rainbow-trout.depth: Top 20 ft in cold weather; deeper in summer

regs.season: Stocked Nov–Apr at Irvine Lake; verify current schedule

rigs.name: PowerBait on a sliding sinker
rigs.detail: 1/4 oz sliding egg sinker, 24" 6 lb fluoro leader, #14 treble with a ball of chartreuse or rainbow PowerBait floating just off the bottom near the inlet. Classic and deadly on planted fish.

rigs.name: Kastmaster or small spoon, trolled
rigs.detail: 1/4–3/8 oz gold or silver Kastmaster trolled slowly at 1.5–2 mph near the surface on a long line. Cover water to find the school.

rigs.name: Live nightcrawler under a float
rigs.detail: Whole nightcrawler suspended 3–6 ft under a bobber drifted in the current near the inlet — works best early morning.

bluegill.cast: Along dock edges, reed beds & rocky shallows; short casts with ultra-light tackle

rigs.name: Float rig, cricket or waxworm
rigs.detail: Small bobber 2–3 ft up, #8 hook with a live cricket or waxworm; best bait for consistent action. Let it sit and wait for the dip.

rigs.name: Small jig, 1/32 oz
rigs.detail: Tiny tube jig or marabou jig on ultra-light gear — panfish crack, and they hit it fast.

rigs.name: Nightcrawler piece
rigs.detail: Small chunk of nightcrawler on a #10 hook with a split shot; works all day.

striped-bass.cast: Deep structure near the dam, rocky points & channel bends at dawn/dusk

rigs.name: Swimbait, large profile
rigs.detail: 6–9" paddle-tail or soft-body bait on a 1–2 oz head worked slowly near the bottom along rocky structure. Stripers are ambush feeders.

rigs.name: Live crawfish / mudsucker
rigs.detail: Hooked through the tail on a 3/0 hook with a light sinker; incredibly effective near rocky bottom and structure.

rigs.name: Cut mackerel or sardine
rigs.detail: Bottom-soaked on a sliding sinker rig at known striper haunts — great at night.
